meta.title: Mohanad Zahed — Frontend Architect
meta.description: Mohanad Zahed — Software Architect Frontend / Senior Frontend Engineer. Angular, React, micro-frontends, headless commerce.

hero.name: Mohanad Zahed
hero.title: Frontend Architect
hero.tagline: +8 years of passion for clean architecture, modern code, and workflows

about.heading: About me
about.body: I am a Frontend Software Architect with a passion for clean architecture and modern code. For over 8 years, I have been developing Angular applications for insurance, telecommunications, retail, and public sectors. My focus has primarily been on building enterprise e-commerce solutions with SAP Commerce, developing enterprise management systems, driving modularization, and migrating legacy systems to modern headless platforms. With my full-stack knowledge, I communicate effectively with backend teams and design end-to-end CI/CD workflows from commit to release. My profile is further complemented by solid expertise in AI-assisted development, which I use to improve efficiency and accelerate project delivery. My extensive experience with agile methods and Scrum also provides me with a strong understanding of processes, quality, and agile delivery.
facts: Languages — Arabic (native) · English (negotiation level) · German (negotiation level)
Degree — 03/2014 B.Sc. Informatics, Amman (officially recognized in Germany)

certificates.heading: Certificates
certificates.subhead: Continuing education in architecture, delivery, and testing.

step.name: STEP — Master Data Management System
step.description:
STEP (Master Data Capture and Maintenance System) is the central master data system of the Federal Employment Agency (Bundesagentur für Arbeit, BA). It includes the recording and maintenance of company and personal data. STEP serves as a central system for almost all BA IT applications that require master data to fulfill their tasks. The master data is provided for different business use cases and user groups via REST endpoints, Kafka events, file interfaces, service interfaces, and client applications.

In addition, STEP is responsible for the management and maintenance of company data for reporting purposes in the social insurance system, which falls under the responsibility of the organizational unit BNS (Business Number Serviceteam, organizationally assigned to the Employment Agency Saarbrücken). Another component of STEP is MAZ (proof and reporting of non-contributory periods for the pension insurance system).

As part of its implementation, in addition to the IT standards commonly used within the BA, adjustments are also made to ensure compliance with data protection and information security requirements, as well as accessibility regulations.
tasks: Locating, analyzing, and fixing software defects
Supporting the creation of (functional) concepts and solution designs
Ensuring Continuous Integration / Continuous Delivery
Applying the DevSecOps approach
Applying agile practices
Providing 3rd level support
Performing test analysis and creating and maintaining manual component tests, system tests, and end-to-end tests
Implementing new REST endpoints as well as adapting and further developing existing interfaces and Kafka-based event processes
Further development of GUI components
Conducting code reviews and refactoring to improve code quality
Ensuring accessibility

svlfg.name: B2B Insurance Customer Portal Modernization
svlfg.description:
As part of this project, an existing B2B insurance customer portal, originally based on SAP Commerce (Hybris), was modernized and restructured. The previously monolithic system architecture was replaced with a decoupled, headless approach, with the frontend redeveloped using Angular. The backend initially remained in the SAP Hybris / Spring Boot environment, with controllers adapted to provide RESTful API endpoints according to the OpenAPI standard. Work was carried out following the Scrum framework in one-month sprints.

The long-term goal of the project was to gradually migrate from SAP Commerce (Hybris) to a modern, JHipster-based solution—primarily because SAP Commerce served mainly as a technological foundation without using its e-commerce functionalities. The migration also aimed to achieve a significant reduction in licensing costs.

The restructuring was intended to improve maintainability, promote frontend-backend independence, and make the platform future-proof for extensions and integrations.
tasks: Migration of the Angular application to a monorepo using Nx to structure and share libraries across multiple applications.
Establishment and enforcement of best practices in frontend architecture and code style
Support and technical guidance for the internal frontend team
Conducting code reviews and providing technical feedback on merge requests within the development team
Close collaboration with the backend team to integrate and utilize OpenAPI-based REST APIs
Implementation of UI components based on the new style guide in close cooperation with the design team
Ensuring accessibility, responsive design, and compliance with UX/UI guidelines
Performing manual component and UI testing
Development of GUI components with a focus on usability and performance
Documentation of source code and UI components
Setup and configuration of the Angular pipeline to support continuous integration

mobilcom-ecare.name: mobilcom-debitel eCare User Dashboard Relaunch
mobilcom-ecare.description:
As part of a comprehensive relaunch, the existing eCare self-service platform was modernized to deliver a faster, more user-friendly, and visually appealing solution. Key focuses included performance optimization, a new responsive design, and the addition of features that provide users with a clearer overview of personal data such as contract information, usage behavior, and account details.

The mobilcom-debitel eCare user dashboard was fully redeveloped based on the existing Angular architecture. The project was organized as an Nx monorepo and consistently used GraphQL for backend communication. Additionally, the dashboard was functionally enhanced to dynamically display personalized content such as marketing campaigns, product recommendations, and special offers in a user-centric manner.

Development was carried out in an agile Scrum setup in close collaboration with UX designers, the frontend team, and marketing stakeholders. The technical focus was on maintaining a clean and maintainable architecture, high component reusability, and scalability for future requirements.
tasks: Implementation of the new eCare dashboard page to display contracts, data usage, invoices, marketing content, and other information
Collaboration with designers and project managers to deliver the desired user interface
Creation and maintenance of Jira tickets for task planning and organization
Integration of the new dashboard into the existing Angular application in coordination with the development team
Development using Angular, TypeScript, HTML, and SCSS within an Nx monorepo architecture
Retrieval of backend data via GraphQL with a focus on performance and maintainability
Implementation of new UI components according to the style guide
Ensuring accessibility, responsive design, and compliance with UX/UI guidelines
Execution of end-to-end (E2E) tests
Development of GUI components with a focus on usability

emporix.name: Agnostic Integration SDK for Emporix
emporix.description: The goal of the project was to develop an agnostic integration SDK that can be seamlessly embedded into any frontend application and provides an immediately usable connection to the Emporix e-commerce platform. The SDK was designed to be technology-independent to ensure maximum flexibility and reusability. The official integration boilerplate from Alokai served as the foundation to guarantee standardization and maintainability.
tasks: Development of TypeScript modules in the integration SDK to handle login processes as well as product and product list queries
Implementation and maintenance of the SDK as reusable npm packages
Publication and versioning of the packages in the internal GitLab registry
Ensuring framework-agnostic design and easy integration into various frontend applications

haba.name: Online-shop Performance Optimization (HABA Play & HABA Pro)
haba.description: The goal of the project was to improve the performance of the HABA Play and HABA Pro online shops while reducing maintenance and operational costs. The sites suffered from long loading times and complex dependencies on the SAP Composable Storefront. At the client’s request, the SAP Composable Storefront was to be removed without losing key functionalities.
tasks: Analysis of the existing shops to identify loading issues and unnecessary JavaScript
Implementation of optimizations to reduce load times, e.g., through lazy loading
Support in removing the SAP Composable Storefront at the client’s request
Migration of key functionalities into a standalone, reusable library
Simplification and reduction of the codebase to lower maintenance effort and file size
Collaboration with the development team and stakeholders to ensure a smooth transition without loss of functionality

ottos-sherpa.name: Modern online shops on SAP Commerce Cloud and Composable Storefront
ottos-sherpa.description: The goal of the project was to develop modern online shops for ottos.ch and Sherpa Outdoors based on SAP Commerce Cloud and the SAP Composable Storefront. The migration involved moving from the legacy SAP Hybris system to a headless storefront architecture using SAP Composable Storefront. A unified Angular frontend codebase was implemented, customized for each brand through different themes and configurations. The project was managed using the Scrum methodology and carried out in close collaboration with Valantic (Zurich), Medienwerft (Hamburg), Team Neusta (Bremen), and Maify GmbH (Mühlingen).
tasks: Frontend project leadership and technical support for the teams from Team Neusta and Medienwerft in using SAP Composable Storefront
Creation of technical subtasks and assignment of Jira tickets to structure and simplify onboarding
Review of team members’ code and merge requests, and active contribution to the code
Close collaboration and coordination with the backend team, product manager, and product owner
Ensuring adherence to best practices, code standards, and quality in the frontend
Support in technical decision-making and problem-solving throughout the project
Implementation of UI components based on the new style guide in close collaboration with the design team
Ensuring accessibility, responsive design, and compliance with UX/UI guidelines
Performing manual component and end-to-end (E2E) testing
Development of GUI components with a focus on usability and performance
Setup and configuration of the Angular pipeline to support continuous integration

landgard.name: B2B Online Shops for Landgard and Ordertage
landgard.description: The goal of the project was to develop modern online shops for Landgard and Ordertage based on SAP Commerce Cloud and the SAP Composable Storefront. A unified Angular frontend codebase was used, customized for each brand through different themes and configurations. The project was executed using the Scrum methodology.
tasks: Implementation of components and functionalities in the SAP Composable Storefront
Customization of the unified Angular codebase for each brand through configuration and theming
Close collaboration with the backend team to integrate interfaces and data flows
Coordination with the product owner to implement business requirements
Participation in sprint planning, reviews, and daily meetings within the Scrum team
Implementation of UI components based on the new style guide in close collaboration with the design team
Ensuring accessibility, responsive design, and compliance with UX/UI guidelines
Development of GUI components with a focus on usability and performance

spar.name: Online-shop Accessibility Refit (SPAR Hungary & Italy)
spar.description: The goal of the project was to make the online shops of SPAR Hungary and SPAR Italy fully accessible. The existing shops were based on SAP Commerce (Hybris) with a JSP frontend that did not meet current digital accessibility standards. To address this, a large portion of the existing code was revised and refactored to comply with accessibility guidelines and ensure a better user experience for all customers. The project was executed using the Scrum methodology.
tasks: Analysis of the existing JSP frontend for accessibility issues
Refactoring and adjustment of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript to comply with accessibility guidelines
Conducting accessibility tests and using relevant tools for quality assurance
Supporting the team in implementing accessible standards within the Scrum process

daw.name: 13 Online Shops for the DAW Group
daw.industry: Construction chemicals / Paint and coatings industry
daw.description: The goal of the project was to develop a new e-commerce system based on SAP Commerce Hybris, enabling users to customize colors to configure the right product. The system was designed as a central solution and is used across 13 different online shops, each with its own CMS content, product assortment, and individual themes. The implementation was carried out within a Scrum process.
tasks: Implementation of frontend features reusable across all 13 online shops
Close collaboration with the backend team
Coordination with the product owner to clarify business requirements
Adaptation of components to different CMS content, product data, and themes
Implementation of UI components based on the new style guide in close collaboration with the design team

dachdecker.name: Roofers' Online Shop
dachdecker.description: The goal of the project was to develop a new online shop based on SAP Commerce Hybris, specifically tailored for roofing supplies. The shop was individually configured and thematically customized to meet the requirements of the roofing industry. The implementation was carried out using an agile Scrum methodology.
tasks: Further development and implementation of new features for the existing online shop
Fixing bugs and technical issues in the frontend
Collaboration with the backend team to integrate new functionalities
Coordination with the product owner to prioritize requirements
Implementation of UI components based on the new style guide in close collaboration with the design team

frosta.name: FRoSTA Digital Work Instructions
frosta.description: The goal of the project was to develop a mobile application for the digital presentation of work and machine instructions. The content included videos, images, PDFs, and text descriptions, designed to visually and clearly explain work steps to employees. The content was managed via a separate web application accessible only to authorized users. The project included a native Android app, an Angular-based CMS, and a REST API based on JAX-RS, connected to a Microsoft SQL Server.
tasks: Frontend: Implementation of the mobile app using native Android to display work instructions with videos, images, PDFs, and text
Frontend: Development of an Angular 6 CMS web application for content management by authorized users
Frontend: Design of a user-friendly UI for content display and management
Backend: Development of RESTful web services using JAX-RS to provide content for the app and CMS
Backend: Integration and management of content via a Microsoft SQL Server database
Backend: Ensuring data consistency and performance in coordination with frontend and database
Technical alignment with the product owner to implement requirements

lsw.name: LSW Warehouse Picking
lsw.description: The goal of the project was to develop a mobile application for Android and iOS tablets, used by warehouse staff for order picking and quality assurance. The app allows scanning product barcodes, displaying relevant information, performing quality checks, and completing work orders. Processes are confirmed with the employee’s signature, and order data is then transmitted from the backend to the connected SAP ERP system via the SAP Java Connector (JCo). The implementation was based on Ionic for the frontend and JAX-RS web services with Microsoft SQL Server in the backend.
tasks: Frontend: Development of a cross-platform mobile app using Ionic for Android and iOS
Frontend: Integration of barcode scanning functionality to capture product data in the warehouse
Frontend: Display of product-relevant information
Frontend: Implementation of digital signature functionality to confirm work orders
Backend: Development of RESTful web services using JAX-RS for integration with the SAP ERP system
Backend: Connection to a SQL Server database for management and storage of order data
Backend: Processing and forwarding of completed orders to the ERP system
Backend: Ensuring stable communication between the app and backend system

contact.headline: Let's build something.
